mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand that compresses corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint negative signals yield positive forward returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly contractions in industrial production below negative 0.5 percent reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative skew during industrial contractions (t-stat > -1.5) across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 3.5 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0 percent, markets price in aggressive future tightening that compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered months, or the inflation-rate spread loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls above 200000 signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth above 200000 correlates with negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 10-year sample, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales decline while GDP remains positive, indicates weakening consumer momentum that precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in more than 60% of cases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign during late-cycle phases in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in industrial production combined with stable employment levels reflect healthy economic throughput that supports short-term equity risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined positive signals yield negative forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the joint effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.15) in walk-forward evaluation.
